From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Sports management company

Roc Nation Sports

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Company type | [Sports management company](/wiki/Management "Management") |
| Industry | [Management](/wiki/Management "Management") |
| Founded | 2013 |
| Founder | [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") |
| Headquarters | [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), U.S. |
| Area served | Worldwide |
| Key people | [Juan Perez](/w/index.php?title=Juan_Perez_(business_man)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Juan Perez (business man) (page does not exist)") ([President](/wiki/President_(corporate_title) "President (corporate title)"))[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) |
| [Parent](/wiki/Parent_company "Parent company") | [Roc Nation](/wiki/Roc_Nation "Roc Nation") |
| Website | [Official website](http://rocnation.com/sports) |

**Roc Nation Sports** is the sports management division of [Roc Nation](/wiki/Roc_Nation "Roc Nation").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Roc_Nation_Sports&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Roc Nation Sports was founded by [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z") in early 2013 in partnership with [Creative Artists Agency](/wiki/Creative_Artists_Agency "Creative Artists Agency"), a prominent talent agency based in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Roc Nation Sports' first major move was signing [New York Yankees](/wiki/New_York_Yankees "New York Yankees") [All-Star](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball_All-Star_Game "Major League Baseball All-Star Game") second baseman [Robinson Canó](/wiki/Robinson_Can%C3%B3 "Robinson Canó").[[4]](#cite_note-4) They have also signed [New York Giants](/wiki/New_York_Giants "New York Giants") receiver [Victor Cruz](/wiki/Victor_Cruz_(American_football) "Victor Cruz (American football)") and [WNBA](/wiki/WNBA "WNBA") prospect [Skylar Diggins-Smith](/wiki/Skylar_Diggins-Smith "Skylar Diggins-Smith") to Roc Nation Sports.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) On June 20, 2013 it was revealed that Jay-Z was officially certified as a [sports agent](/wiki/Sports_agent "Sports agent") for the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") and [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball").[[7]](#cite_note-7) On May 1, 2014 Roc Nation Sports added [James Young](/wiki/James_Young_(basketball) "James Young (basketball)") of the [Kentucky Wildcats](/wiki/Kentucky_Wildcats "Kentucky Wildcats").[[8]](#cite_note-8) In November 2014, [Dallas Cowboys](/wiki/Dallas_Cowboys "Dallas Cowboys") wide receiver [Dez Bryant](/wiki/Dez_Bryant "Dez Bryant") would also join the company. On January 3, 2018, it was announced that Penn State running back [Saquon Barkley](/wiki/Saquon_Barkley "Saquon Barkley") had signed with Roc Nation Sports prior to entering the [2018 NFL draft](/wiki/2018_NFL_draft "2018 NFL draft").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Roc Nation Sports currently boasts a roster with over 100 athletes across baseball, basketball, American football, soccer, rugby, and e-sports. They also have a burgeoning broadcaster division.
